data.title: Tesla Shares Slide on Lower Q1 Delivery Outlook Across Key Markets
data.description:
Tesla shares declined more than 3% Friday as concerns mounted that the company's first-quarter global deliveries will fall short of expectations.

A report from RBC Capital Markets estimates Q1 deliveries at 364,000 vehicles, below the consensus forecast of 398,000. The shortfall is attributed to a temporary halt in Model Y production as Tesla prepares a refreshed version of the vehicle.

The timing of the refresh may have dampened demand, with prospective buyers reportedly waiting for the updated model. RBC also pointed to the likely release of a more affordable Tesla vehicle in Q2 as another factor contributing to reduced near-term sales.

According to RBC's regional breakdown, Q1 deliveries are expected to fall 5.4% in the U.S., decline nearly 48% in China, and drop close to 20% in Europe.

Despite the weaker outlook, RBC maintained its outperform rating and $320 price target on Tesla, citing the company's long-term potential in the electric vehicle market as well as its positions in energy, software, and artificial intelligence.

Tesla shares have declined about 36% over the past three months, reflecting both delivery concerns and shifting investor sentiment.

Innoviz said it will monitor its stock price during the grace period and consider its options to meet the minimum bid price requirement.

data.title: Swedish journalist jailed in Turkey on terrorism, insult charges
data.description:
Key points:
  • Medin charged with terrorism and insulting Erdogan
  • Sweden demands consular access for Medin
  • Medin's past conflict zone reporting cited by Turkey

Swedish journalist Kaj Joakim Medin, who was in Turkey to cover protests against Istanbul Mayor Ekrem Imamoglu's arrest, was jailed pending trial on terrorism charges, Turkey's Ankara Chief Public Prosecutor's Office said on Friday.

Medin was detained in Istanbul as part of an investigation launched after a protest in Stockholm in January 2023, where a mannequin resembling President Tayyip Erdogan was hung outside the city hall.

Authorities said Medin was among 15 suspects identified for organizing, promoting, or being linked to the demonstration.

Medin was charged with "insulting the president" and "membership in a terrorist organization."

His employer, Dagens ETC newspaper, had raised concerns over his whereabouts after he was unreachable for two days.

Dagens ETC Editor-in-Chief Andreas Gustavsson told Swedish TV4 that Medin had done nothing wrong and was merely being punished for doing his job as a reporter.

Turkish authorities also cited Medin's past reporting from conflict zones in Syria, Iraq and southeastern Turkey between 2014 and 2017.

He was formally arrested via video conference by an Ankara court after being questioned in Istanbul.

Sweden has asked the Turkish ambassador to Stockholm to clarify the allegations against Medin and to demand that he receive urgent access to consular services, Swedish Foreign Minister Maria Malmer Stenergard told public broadcaster SVT.

data.title: Wheat Futures Fall on Black Sea NegotiationsDaily Grain Highlights
data.description:
By Kirk Maltais

  • Wheat for May delivery fell 0.7% to $5.28 1/2 a bushel on the Chicago Board of Trade on Friday, with analysts and traders anticipating that negotiations to temporarily halt fighting will allow export shipments to pass through the Black Sea.
  • Soybeans for May delivery rose 0.5% to $10.17 1/2 a bushel.
  • Corn for May delivery rose 0.7% to $4.56 1/4 a bushel.

HIGHLIGHTS

Wheat in Retreat: The decline in wheat prices led the CBOT lower as negotiations to halt hostilities between Russia and Ukraine weigh on prices. "Though the resumption of the grain accord brokered by Turkey is uncertain, the possibility of increased Black Sea output and exports is also seen as a negative price driver, especially towards wheat," Ocean State Research's Joel Karlin said. The Black Sea Grain Initiative came into force in July 2022, and lasted for about a year.

Fear Factor: Worries about how reciprocal tariffs expected on April 2 will affect demand is weighing on the agricultural complex. "The uncertainty of it all has fear as the dominant factor in the markets heading into the weekend," said Arlan Suderman of StoneX in a note. In particular, Suderman is watching for retaliatory tariffs from Mexico, which would have a big effect on U.S. grain exports. "Retaliatory tariffs by Mexico on Ag commodities could crush demand for vital commodities."

INSIGHT

Drought May Ease: Data tracking dryness in U.S. crop-growing areas is gaining importance with analysts and traders, with extreme drought conditions spreading in the Northern Plains and in Texas, according to data from the U.S. Drought Monitor on Thursday. While the data show expanded areas of drought across the Corn Belt, rainfall is expected to hit the Eastern Corn Belt soon. "7-Day precipitation accumulation has been boosted to 2-4 inches east of the Mississippi River," Price Futures Group's Daniel Flynn said in a note. "This matches what's needed to ease/end drought there, and so is viewed as beneficial."

Unnerving Data: A hotter-than-expected February core PCE reading sparked new concerns about stagflation and the role tariffs may play going forward. "We are moving in the wrong direction and the concern is that tariffs threaten higher prices, which mean the inflation prints are going to remain hot," ING Economics said in a note. "This will constrain the Fed's ability to deliver further interest rate cuts." These concerns have pushed commodity futures into the red across the board, except for precious metals and copper, with gold surging above the $3,100 per troy ounce.

AHEAD

  • The USDA will release its weekly Grain Export Inspections report at 11 a.m. ET Monday.
  • The USDA will release its yearly Prospective Planting report at noon ET Monday.
  • The USDA will release its quarterly Grain Stocks report at noon ET Monday.

Write to Kirk Maltais at kirk.maltais@wsj.com

data.title: STRATUS PROPERTIES INC SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Stratus Properties Inc., a real estate development and management company primarily operating in the Austin, Texas area, has released its 2024 10-K report. The report highlights a significant financial turnaround, strategic initiatives aimed at long-term growth, and the challenges the company faces in the current economic environment.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenues: Stratus Properties Inc. reported total revenues of $54.183 million, a substantial increase from the previous year's $17.270 million. This growth was driven by higher performance in both Real Estate Operations and Leasing Operations.

Operating Loss: The company recorded an operating loss of $(2.155) million, a marked improvement from the previous year's operating loss of $(16.946) million, indicating better cost management and increased revenues.

Net Income (Loss) and Total Comprehensive Income (Loss) Attributable to Common Stockholders: Stratus achieved a net income of $1.956 million, compared to a loss of $(14.807) million in the prior year, highlighting a significant turnaround in profitability.

Basic and Diluted Net Income (Loss) Per Share Attributable to Common Stockholders: Both basic and diluted net income per share were $0.24, compared to $(1.85) in the previous year, reflecting improved earnings performance.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: Stratus Properties Inc. generated revenue from two main segments: Real Estate Operations, which brought in $34.9 million, and Leasing Operations, contributing $19.3 million for the year ended December 31, 2024.

Geographical Performance: The company primarily operates in the Austin, Texas area and other select markets in Texas. Any significant economic downturn in these regions could potentially impact Stratus' business operations.

Sales Units: During 2024, Stratus sold multiple real estate units, including one Amarra Drive Phase III lot and five Amarra Villas homes, contributing to the company's operational performance.

New Production Launches: The company continued the development of the Holden Hills Phase 1 Project, which is part of its final large residential development within the Barton Creek community in Austin, Texas.

Future Outlook: Stratus is focused on the development and sale of multi-family and single-family residential and commercial real estate properties in the Austin, Texas area and other select markets in Texas. The company is also managing its partnerships and construction projects to optimize returns and manage risks associated with market conditions.

Strategic Initiatives

Strategic Partnerships: Stratus Properties Inc. has engaged in several strategic partnerships for development projects, including Holden Hills, L.P., The Saint George Apartments, L.P., Stratus Block 150, L.P., The Saint June, L.P., and Stratus Kingwood Place, L.P. These partnerships are aimed at developing residential and commercial real estate in Texas, with Stratus holding significant equity interests and potential for increased returns based on performance hurdles.

Capital Management: Stratus has actively managed its capital through various debt arrangements, including construction loans and credit facilities, to finance its development projects. The company has refinanced several loans to improve terms and extend maturities, such as the Kingwood Place loan and the Lantana Place construction loan. Additionally, Stratus has engaged in share repurchase programs, including a $10 million program completed in 2022 and a new $5 million program approved in 2023. The company also declared a special cash dividend of $4.67 per share in 2022.

Future Outlook: Stratus plans to continue its strategic focus on real estate development in Texas, leveraging its partnerships and refinancing efforts to optimize capital structure and enhance returns. The company aims to maintain compliance with financial covenants and explore opportunities for further capital investment, while managing its debt-to-asset ratio and maintaining a strong liquidity position. Future initiatives may include additional share repurchases and dividend payments, subject to lender approvals.

Challenges and Risks

Market and Economic Risks: The company faces significant challenges due to the decline in demand and prices for residential real estate in the Austin area, exacerbated by high inflation and interest rates. The enactment of Texas Senate Bill 2038 (the ETJ Law) in 2023 introduces regulatory uncertainty, as ongoing litigation may affect the company's development plans and property valuations.

Operational Risks: Stratus is exposed to risks from its reliance on joint ventures for capital, which may lead to conflicts of interest or financial obligations if partners default. The company has experienced increased costs due to inflation, higher borrowing costs, and supply chain constraints, which may continue to impact profitability and cash flow. Additionally, operational risks include potential delays and cost overruns in development projects, labor shortages, and increased construction costs.

Environmental and Catastrophic Risks: The company also faces risks from adverse weather conditions and other catastrophic events, which may be amplified by climate change and could disrupt operations or damage properties.

Management Strategies: Management acknowledges the challenges posed by elevated inflation and interest rates, which have delayed projects and impacted the ability to raise capital. The company is adjusting its development plans in response to the ETJ Law and is incurring costs aligned with revised plans, subject to the risk of the law being invalidated. Management is also focused on maintaining strong relationships with key tenants to mitigate risks in leasing operations.

Market Risks: The company is exposed to market risks from variable interest rate debt, which could increase interest costs if rates rise. The company typically does not hedge against interest rate changes, except for specific loans where required by lenders. Additionally, the company faces risks from potential changes in U.S. trade policies, such as tariffs on lumber imports, which could increase construction costs.

data.title: PEOPLES FINANCIAL SERVICES CORP. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Peoples Financial Services Corp., a bank holding company operating through its subsidiary Peoples Security Bank and Trust Company, has released its annual 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the current market environment. The company, which serves retail and commercial customers through 39 full-service community banking offices across Pennsylvania, New Jersey, and New York, has detailed significant developments and future outlooks in its latest filing.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Interest Income: $211.46 million, reflecting a significant increase from the previous year due to higher interest and fees on loans.
  • Net Interest Income: $115.99 million, showing growth driven by increased interest income despite higher interest expenses.
  • Net Income: $8.50 million, a decrease from the previous year, impacted by higher noninterest expenses and provision for credit losses.
  • Basic Earnings Per Share: $1.00, reflecting the impact of increased shares outstanding due to the acquisition of FNCB Bancorp, Inc.
  • Diluted Earnings Per Share: $0.99, consistent with the basic earnings per share, indicating minimal dilution effects.

Business Highlights

  • Nature of Operations: Peoples Financial Services Corp. operates as a bank holding company providing a full range of financial services through its subsidiary, Peoples Security Bank and Trust Company. The company serves retail and commercial customers through 39 full-service community banking offices across Pennsylvania, New Jersey, and New York.
  • Product Lines: The Bank's primary products include loans to small and medium-sized businesses, equipment financing and leasing, one-to-four family residential mortgages, and consumer loans. Deposit products offered include checking accounts, savings accounts, money market accounts, and certificates of deposit.
  • Geographical Performance: The company operates in several counties across Pennsylvania, New Jersey, and New York, facing competition from both local and national financial institutions. The competitive landscape is influenced by changes in legal and regulatory guidelines and economic conditions.
  • Business Combination: On July 1, 2024, Peoples Financial Services Corp. completed the acquisition of FNCB Bancorp, Inc., expanding its branch network and market share in northeastern Pennsylvania. This merger is expected to create meaningful value for shareholders and enhance the company's market presence.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to leverage the synergies from the FNCB acquisition to enhance operational efficiencies and expand its customer base. The integration of FNCB is expected to provide increased trading liquidity and dividends for shareholders.
  • Operational Strategy: The company focuses on prudent underwriting practices, conservative debt service coverage, and loan-to-value ratios to manage credit risk effectively. It also emphasizes lending to seasoned real estate owners and managers, often with personal guarantees of repayment.
  • Regulatory Environment: Peoples Financial Services Corp. and its subsidiaries are subject to regulations from federal and state agencies, undergoing periodic examinations to ensure compliance with banking laws and regulations.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Initiatives: The company completed the acquisition of FNCB Bancorp, Inc., expanding its branch network and market share in northeastern Pennsylvania. This strategic merger was aimed at enhancing shareholder value, increasing trading liquidity, and providing a stronger cultural alignment with a commitment to shareholders, customers, employees, and communities.
  • Capital Management: The company issued 2,935,456 shares of common stock valued at $45.54 per share as part of the merger consideration for FNCB Bancorp, Inc. Additionally, the company declared cash dividends of $2.06 per common share in 2024, up from $1.64 in 2023. The company also engaged in stock compensation activities, including the issuance of restricted stock and retirement of shares.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue integrating FNCB's operations to realize synergies and economies of scale. It will focus on maintaining a strong capital position to support future growth and shareholder returns, while also exploring opportunities for further strategic acquisitions and expansions.

Challenges and Risks

The company faces significant interest rate risk (IRR) due to its lending, investing, and deposit-gathering activities. This risk is primarily associated with changes in interest rates, which can affect the economic value of assets, liabilities, and off-balance sheet items. A high degree of IRR may lead to lower earnings, impaired liquidity, and capital positions, increasing the risk of insolvency. The Asset Liability Committee (ALCO) is tasked with managing this risk by establishing policy guidelines and regularly reporting to the Board of Directors. The company uses various financial instruments, including interest rate swaps, caps, and floors, to manage IRR.

The ALCO employs income simulation to measure the impact of interest rate shifts on net interest income over 24-month and 60-month horizons. As of December 31, 2024, the company's exposure to changing interest rates remained within established tolerance levels, although a sustained falling rate environment poses the greatest potential risk to net interest income over the longer term. The company's interest rate risk position was less asset-sensitive at the end of 2024 compared to 2023, due to changes in the balance sheet mix and a decrease in floating rate assets. The ALCO regularly reviews interest rate shift scenarios, including steepening or flattening yield curves and parallel changes in interest rates up to 400 basis points, to evaluate exposure and develop strategies to manage this risk. The simulation model indicates that a 100 basis point increase in interest rates would result in a 0.2% decrease in projected net interest income for the 12 months ending December 31, 2024.

SEC Filing: PEOPLES FINANCIAL SERVICES CORP. [ PFIS ] - 10-K - Mar. 28, 2025

data.title: NU RIDE INC. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Nu Ride Inc., formerly known as Lordstown Motors Corp., has released its 2024 10-K report, detailing the company's financial and operational performance following its emergence from Chapter 11 bankruptcy. The report highlights significant improvements in financial metrics, strategic initiatives aimed at future growth, and the challenges and risks the company faces in its new phase of operations.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Sales: $0 million, reflecting the cessation of operations and sales following the Chapter 11 bankruptcy filing.
  • Cost of Sales: $0 million, as there were no sales or production activities during the year ended December 31, 2024.
  • Loss from Operations: $(10.9) million, significantly reduced from the previous year's loss of $(348.9) million due to the cessation of operations and reduced expenses.
  • Net Loss: $(8.1) million, a substantial improvement from the $(343.1) million net loss in 2023, primarily due to the absence of impairment charges and reduced operating expenses.
  • Net Loss Attributable to Common Shareholders: $(10.8) million, compared to $(345.6) million in 2023, reflecting the impact of accrued preferred stock dividends.
  • Net Loss Per Share: $(0.67), improved from $(21.67) in 2023, due to the reduced net loss and the impact of the reverse stock split.

Business Highlights

  • Bankruptcy Filing and Asset Sale: On June 27, 2023, Lordstown Motors Corp. filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy, ceasing production and sales of its flagship vehicle, the Endurance. The company entered into an Asset Purchase Agreement with LandX Motors Inc. to sell assets related to electric light-duty vehicles, which closed on October 27, 2023, for $10.2 million.
  • Post-Bankruptcy Operations: Following emergence from bankruptcy in March 2024, the company, now named Nu Ride Inc., focuses on claims administration, litigation, and exploring potential business opportunities. The company has no full-time employees, with executive management provided by M3 Partners.
  • Foxconn Litigation: The company is engaged in litigation against Foxconn for alleged fraudulent and tortious conduct, with significant claims surviving a motion to dismiss. The outcome of this litigation could impact the company's financial recovery.
  • Production and Sales Cessation: Production of the Endurance vehicle ended in June 2023, with only 35 vehicles sold in the first nine months of 2023. The company repurchased most of these vehicles as part of the bankruptcy proceedings.
  • Future Business Opportunities: The company may explore strategic alternatives or business combinations to create value, although no specific agreements or discussions are currently in place.
  • Operational Focus: The company's primary operations include resolving bankruptcy claims, pursuing litigation, and identifying potential transactions to leverage its net operating loss carryforwards.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Reorganization and New Board: Nu Ride Inc. emerged from Chapter 11 bankruptcy on March 14, 2024, under a reorganization plan that included the appointment of a new Board of Directors and the termination of all remaining full-time employees.
  • Capital Management: Upon emergence from bankruptcy, Nu Ride Inc. had cash and cash equivalents of approximately $23.1 million and short-term investments of approximately $6.4 million, excluding restricted short-term investments of approximately $23.4 million. The company established a $45 million reserve for allowed and disputed claims of general unsecured creditors. The company also issued Series A Convertible Preferred Stock to Foxconn, which remains outstanding and unimpaired, with accrued dividends of $2.7 million for 2024.
  • Future Outlook: Nu Ride Inc. plans to explore potential business opportunities, including strategic alternatives or business combinations, to preserve the value of its net operating losses (NOLs). The company anticipates that the prosecution of claims and causes of action and the evaluation and pursuit of potential strategic alternatives will be costly, complex, and risky. The outcome of the Foxconn Litigation and the resolution of significant contingent claims will be critical to the company's future liquidity and capital resources.

Challenges and Risks

  • Limited Operations and Assets: The company faces significant risks due to its limited operations and assets, which may be exhausted without additional funding or successful litigation outcomes. The company's financial condition is precarious, with no significant income-generating assets and reliance on cash reserves and potential litigation recoveries.
  • Foxconn Litigation: The Foxconn Litigation is a major focus, but its outcome is uncertain and could result in substantial expenses without guaranteed recoveries.
  • Net Operating Loss Carryforwards (NOLs): The company's ability to utilize its NOLs is uncertain and may be limited by ownership changes or IRS challenges.
  • Dependence on New Management: Operational risks include dependence on a new Board of Directors and management team, with challenges in attracting and retaining qualified personnel. The company relies heavily on consultants and third-party service providers, which poses risks if their services are lost or disrupted.
  • Regulatory Risks: The company may face significant uninsured losses due to reduced insurance coverage post-bankruptcy. Changes in laws or regulations could adversely affect the company's operations and financial condition.
  • Financial Stability: The company's financial results are not comparable to historical performance due to the restructuring and bankruptcy proceedings. The company's liquidity is dependent on resolving claims and realizing value from litigation and remaining assets.

SEC Filing: NU RIDE INC. [ NRDE ] - 10-K - Mar. 28, 2025

data.title: Lifeloc Technologies, Inc SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Lifeloc Technologies, Inc., a Colorado-based developer, manufacturer, and marketer of portable hand-held and fixed station breathalyzers and related accessories, has released its annual 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the current market environment.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenues: $8.54 million, a decrease of 9% from the previous year, primarily due to satisfying pent-up demand and inflationary pressures on customer budgets.

Gross Profit: $3.45 million, a decrease of 15% from the previous year, impacted by lower product sales and inflationary effects on costs.

Operating (Loss): $(1.41) million, compared to an operating loss of $(191,135) in the previous year, driven by increased research and development expenses and sales and marketing efforts.

Net Income (Loss): $(1.05) million, a significant decrease from a net income of $205,614 in the previous year, due to changes in gross profit and increased operating expenses.

Net Income (Loss) Per Share, Basic: $(0.41), compared to $0.08 in the previous year, reflecting the overall decline in net income.

Business Highlights

Business Overview: Lifeloc Technologies, Inc. operates in major segments of the breath alcohol testing instrument market, including law enforcement, workplace, corrections, OEM, and consumer markets. The company's primary products include the FC Series and Workplace Series of portable breath alcohol testers, which are DOT approved and designed for law enforcement and workplace alcohol testing programs. Lifeloc also offers supplies, accessories, services, and training to support customers' alcohol testing programs.

New Product Developments: Lifeloc is working on the commercialization of SpinDx™, a technology for the detection of drugs of abuse, which is expected to revolutionize real-time screening for high abuse drugs. The company plans to introduce this product via beta testing in 2025, with a sales-ready device anticipated in the first half of 2026.

Product Innovations: In 2019, Lifeloc received DOT approval for the LX9 and LT7 base unit alcohol breathalyzers, which have been updated and received further approval in December 2021. The company also introduced the EASYCAL® G2, an automatic calibration station with broader capabilities.

Geographical Performance: Lifeloc sells its products globally through distributors and directly to users, with over 90% of international sales made by local distributors. The company has sold instruments to customers in over 65 countries on six continents.

Future Outlook: Lifeloc anticipates growth in its breath alcohol testing business and plans to expand into new growth areas with the commercialization of new sensing and measurement products. The company expects the introduction of SpinDx™ to improve revenues in 2026 and beyond.

Operational Challenges: The company faced supply chain disruptions in 2023, leading to larger inventories to accommodate longer lead times. Inflationary pressures have increased the cost of raw materials, labor, and freight.

Training and Education: Lifeloc offers comprehensive training programs through Lifeloc University, which includes online training modules and direct training via webcam. The company acquired Superior Training Solutions, Inc. in 2014 to enhance its training offerings.

Real Property: Lifeloc owns its corporate headquarters in Wheat Ridge, Colorado, and leases a portion of the space to a tenant. The company intends to continue leasing the space but may expand its operations into the leased area in the future.

Strategic Initiatives

Strategic Initiatives: Lifeloc Technologies is focusing on expanding its product offerings and market reach. The company is committed to growing its breath alcohol testing business and anticipates the commercialization of new sensing and measurement products, such as the SpinDx technology for drug detection, which is expected to be commercialized in 2026. Additionally, the company is investing in research and development to refine existing products and develop new ones, with a significant focus on the SpinDx platform.

Capital Management: In 2024, Lifeloc Technologies issued a $750,000 subordinated debenture with an 8.25% interest rate, including warrants for 62,500 shares of common stock. The company also completed a private placement of 210,000 shares of common stock, raising $798,000. The term loan secured by a first mortgage on the company's building is being serviced with regular payments. The company maintains a healthy level of sales and marketing expenses to support its growth strategy.

Future Outlook: Lifeloc Technologies expects to see improved revenues in 2026 with the introduction of SpinDx. The company plans to focus on growing sales, increasing gross profits, and enhancing research and development efforts. It anticipates that cash resources will be sufficient to fund operations for the next twelve months under the current operating plan. The company is also exploring opportunities to expand its product offerings and market presence, with a particular emphasis on completing the SpinDx development.

Challenges and Risks

Challenges and Risks: Lifeloc Technologies, Inc. faces significant challenges in its business operations, primarily due to its reliance on a limited number of suppliers for key components such as fuel cells. Any disruption in the supply chain could severely impact production. Additionally, the company operates in a highly competitive market with larger competitors possessing greater resources, which could affect its market position.

Development and Commercialization Risks: There is no assurance that efforts to develop a marijuana breathalyzer will be successful or that significant sales will result if successful.

Economic and Trade Risks: Global economic conditions and potential shifts in U.S. trade policy, including tariffs, could negatively impact the company's operations and supply chain.

Geopolitical Risks: International conflicts and geopolitical instability may disrupt operations and supply chains.

Customer Concentration Risks: A significant portion of revenues is derived from a small number of customers, and the loss of any major customer could adversely affect revenues.

Regulatory Risks: The company is subject to extensive regulatory oversight, and failure to obtain necessary approvals could harm revenues.

Intellectual Property Risks: The company faces risks related to patent infringement and the protection of its intellectual property.

Market Risks: The company's stock lacks a significant trading market, which could lead to volatility and affect investor perceptions.

SpinDx Development: The company is investing heavily in the development of SpinDx technology for drug detection, with commercialization expected in 2026. This represents a significant opportunity but also a risk if market acceptance is delayed.

Supply Chain and Inflation: Supply chain disruptions and inflationary pressures have increased costs, impacting profitability. The company is taking steps to mitigate these effects, including sourcing new vendors and increasing prices.

Financial Performance: The company experienced a net loss in 2024 due to decreased sales and increased costs. Management is focused on growing sales and increasing research and development efforts to support long-term growth.

Market Risks: The company is exposed to market risks, including fluctuations in foreign currency exchange rates and interest rates, which could adversely affect financial results. Additionally, changes in U.S. trade relations, particularly tariffs, could impact the availability of materials and international market opportunities.

SEC Filing: Lifeloc Technologies, Inc [ LCTC ] - 10-K - Mar. 28, 2025

data.title: Investview, Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Investview, Inc., a diversified financial technology and global distribution company, has released its annual Form 10-K report, detailing its financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's activities in financial education, blockchain technology, brokerage services, and health and wellness products.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenue: $52.4 million, a decrease of $15.5 million or 23% from the previous year, primarily due to a contraction in membership, mining, and cryptocurrency revenues.

Operating Costs and Expenses: $50.7 million, a reduction of $12.6 million or 20% from the previous year, driven by lower commissions, cost of sales and services, and general and administrative expenses.

Other Income (Expense): $390,637, a decrease of $14,765 or 4% from the prior year, influenced by a realized gain on cryptocurrency and a loss on settlement with the SEC.

Net Cash Provided by Operating Activities: $8.3 million, utilized for acquisitions, fixed asset purchases, debt payments, and share repurchases, resulting in a year-end cash balance of $22.5 million.

Business Highlights

Financial Education and Technology: Investview operates a financial education division through its subsidiary iGenius, LLC, offering tools, products, and content via a direct selling network. The division provides financial education content, live and recorded sessions, and trade alerts, with a membership-based revenue model.

Blockchain Technology and Crypto Mining: Through SAFETek, LLC, Investview runs a blockchain infrastructure business focused on Bitcoin mining. The company operates 1,916 advanced ASIC miners in Northern Europe, powered by renewable energy. Despite a 78% decrease in Bitcoin production due to the 2024 Bitcoin halving and increased network difficulty, operational improvements have been implemented to enhance efficiency.

Brokerage and Financial Technologies: Investview acquired Opencash Securities LLC, an early-stage broker-dealer, to enter the online retail brokerage market. The platform aims to offer low-cost, commission-free trading and is in advanced development stages, with plans to integrate with the Prodigio trading system for a comprehensive brokerage solution.

Health, Beauty, and Wellness Products: The company expanded into the health, beauty, and wellness market with the acquisition of Renu Laboratories, Inc. The subsidiary, myLife Wellness, focuses on aesthetics, health, nutrition, and cognitive wellness products, distributed through retail and wholesale channels.

Geographical Performance: In 2024, foreign revenues were approximately $42.9 million, while domestic revenues were about $9.5 million, indicating a strong international presence.

Operational Challenges: The company faced a significant contraction in revenues from its Financial Education and Technology and Blockchain Technology and Crypto Mining units due to shifts in consumer behavior post-COVID-19 and the Bitcoin halving event.

Future Outlook: Investview plans to commercialize its Opencash business and expand its health and wellness product line, aiming for revenue growth despite current market challenges. The company is also evaluating strategic hosting infrastructure acquisitions to optimize costs and enhance returns.

Strategic Initiatives

Strategic Initiatives: The company has undertaken several strategic initiatives, including the acquisition of Opencash, an early-stage registered broker-dealer, and Renu Labs, which focuses on health and wellness products. These acquisitions are aimed at diversifying the company's offerings and expanding into new markets. Additionally, the company has been involved in private share repurchase transactions to strategically reduce outstanding capitalization and add shareholder value.

Capital Management: The company has engaged in multiple capital management activities, including private share repurchase transactions at significant discounts to market prices, which have reduced the number of outstanding shares. It has also amended its credit arrangements with DBR Capital to secure more favorable interest rates and extended loan terms. Furthermore, the company has authorized a stock repurchase program to buy back up to $1,000,000 in shares, enhancing shareholder value. The company has also settled an outstanding matter with the SEC, agreeing to pay a penalty and cease further violations.

Future Outlook: Looking ahead, the company anticipates continued challenges in its Financial Education and Technology, and Blockchain Technology and Crypto Mining business units due to macroeconomic conditions and industry-specific factors like Bitcoin Halving. However, it expects potential revenue growth from the commercialization of its Opencash business and expansion in the health and wellness sector. The company plans to maintain its focus on strategic acquisitions and capital management to navigate these challenges and pursue sustainable growth.

Challenges and Risks

Operational Risks: The company's growth plan for its brokerage and financial markets business has been delayed due to complications in acquiring a registered broker-dealer. The acquisition of Opencash in 2024 is expected to aid expansion, but significant infrastructure development and integration challenges remain.

Regulatory Risks: The company is heavily impacted by interested party transactions and regulatory scrutiny, particularly concerning its iGenius business unit, which has faced allegations of unlicensed securities activities in Canada. Settlements with Canadian regulators could lead to similar claims in other jurisdictions.

Market Risks: The volatility of Bitcoin and the inherent risks of the cryptocurrency market pose significant challenges. The April 2024 Bitcoin halving has reduced block rewards, impacting profitability. The company must manage energy costs and supply chain disruptions due to its reliance on a sole power provider in Northern Europe.

Cybersecurity Risks: The company is at risk of cyber-attacks, which could disrupt operations and lead to significant liabilities. The reliance on third-party custodians for Bitcoin storage adds to this risk.

Financial Risks: The company's financial condition could be adversely affected by its convertible note arrangements with DBR Capital, which include high-interest rates and conversion rights that could dilute existing shareholders.

Legal Risks: Ongoing litigation related to credit card receivables and the Total Protection Plus program could result in significant financial liabilities and reputational damage.

Management Strategies: Management is focused on mitigating risks by enhancing operational efficiency, particularly in its blockchain operations, and by addressing regulatory challenges through settlements and compliance measures. The company is also exploring new business opportunities to diversify its revenue streams.

SEC Filing: Investview, Inc. [ INVU ] - 10-K - Mar. 28, 2025

data.title: Hongchang International Co., Ltd SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Hongchang International Co., Ltd, a holding company incorporated in Nevada and operating primarily in China, has released its annual 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the current regulatory environment. The company has made significant strides in expanding its product offerings and operational capabilities, despite facing substantial legal and operational risks due to the evolving regulatory landscape in China.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Revenue: $2.87 million, reflecting an increase due to the launch of a new beef and mutton supplying business at the end of 2024.
  • Gross Profit: $0.18 million, an improvement from the previous year, driven by increased sales despite higher supplier costs.
  • Loss from Operations: $(0.38) million, showing a reduction in loss compared to the previous year, indicating improved operational efficiency.
  • Net Loss: $(0.47) million, an increase in net loss compared to the previous year, primarily due to higher operating expenses and other income (expense) changes.
  • Basic Net Loss Per Share: $(0.00), consistent with the previous year, reflecting the early stage of business operations and ongoing investments.

Business Highlights

  • Corporate Structure and Operations: The company operates as a holding company incorporated in Nevada, conducting its business in China through subsidiaries. It has a direct equity ownership structure without variable interest entity agreements, which is significant given the legal and operational risks associated with operating in China.
  • Regulatory Environment: The company faces substantial legal and operational risks due to the regulatory environment in China, including potential changes in laws and policies that could affect its business operations and the value of its shares. The PRC government has been increasing oversight on overseas securities offerings and foreign investments in China-based companies.
  • Product and Market Expansion: The company has launched a new beef and mutton supplying business at the end of 2024, which contributed to the increase in revenue. This expansion is part of the company's strategy to increase product variety and expand its distribution network both in China and overseas.
  • Operational Challenges: The company is subject to risks related to the Chinese legal system, including uncertainties in the interpretation and application of PRC laws and regulations, which could materially affect its operations.
  • New Production Facilities: The company is involved in the construction of the Hongchang Food Industrial Park, a key project in Fujian Province, China. This facility is expected to enhance the company's operational capabilities upon completion.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue expanding its product offerings and distribution network. It also anticipates potential regulatory changes in China that could impact its operations and market access.
  • Management Changes: There have been several changes in the company's management and board of directors, including the appointment of Mr. Zengqiang Lin as the Chief Executive Officer and President, which may influence the company's strategic direction.
  • Cash Management and Transfers: The company has not experienced any cash transfers among its subsidiaries and the holding company during the fiscal years 2023 and 2024. There are no current plans for dividend distributions to investors.
  • Construction Progress: The Hongchang Food Industrial Park is under construction, with significant capital commitments expected to be paid off within 1-3 years, indicating ongoing investment in infrastructure to support future growth.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Merger and Business Focus: The company completed a merger with Hongchang BVI, making it a wholly-owned subsidiary, and began conducting the principal business of Hongchang Food. This strategic move aligns with the company's focus on expanding its core business in food trade and construction of the Hongchang Food Industrial Park.
  • Capital Management: The company financed its operations and construction projects through a combination of long-term loans and related party loans, totaling over $10 million in financing activities for 2024. There were no equity repurchases or dividends declared during the fiscal years 2023 and 2024. The company also reported a significant increase in total liabilities, primarily due to amounts due to related parties.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue its strategic business development by expanding its product supply range and establishing mature procurement plans to control costs. It also aims to complete the construction of the Hongchang Food Industrial Park, which is expected to enhance its operational capabilities and market presence.

Challenges and Risks

  • Regulatory and Legal Risks: The company operates primarily through its subsidiaries in China, which exposes it to significant legal and operational risks due to the evolving Chinese legal system. The PRC government has substantial influence over the economy, and any changes in regulations or policies could adversely affect the company's business operations and the value of its shares.
  • Operational Risks: The company faces risks related to the supply and pricing of products, which may be affected by various factors, including seasonal fluctuations and macroeconomic conditions. The company is also exposed to risks related to its construction projects, with significant capital expenditure commitments that could impact its financial condition if not managed properly.
  • Market Risks: The company is exposed to market risks, including foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations, which may adversely affect its financial results. The PRC government's control over currency conversion could impact the company's ability to repatriate profits and manage its financial resources effectively.

SEC Filing: Hongchang International Co., Ltd [ HCIL ] - 10-K - Mar. 28, 2025

data.title: US stocks drop as economic data adds to worries
data.description:
Key points:
  • Dow, S&P 500 end down >1%; Nasdaq slides >2%
  • Comm svcs off most among S&P sectors; utilities only gainer
  • Dollar down, crude falls; gold up; bitcoin down ~4%
  • U.S. 10-Year Treasury yield falls to ~4.25%

US STOCKS DROP AS ECONOMIC DATA ADDS TO WORRIES

All three major U.S. stock indexes ended down sharply on Friday, led by a more than 2% drop in the Nasdaq as U.S. economic data added to concerns about tepid growth and high inflation.

U.S. consumer spending rebounded less than expected in February while a measure of underlying prices increased by the most in 13 months, according to Commerce Department data on Friday.

Stripping out the volatile food and energy components, the Personal Consumption Expenditures price index rose 0.4% in February. That was the biggest gain in the so-called core inflation since January 2024 and followed an unrevised 0.3% advance in January.

Worries about the economic outlook have increased with Trump this week unveiling a 25% levy on imported cars and light trucks starting next week.

"We think there's likely to be weakening consumer spending going forward, which may throw cold water on the rising prices,” said Greg Bassuk, CEO at AXS Investments in New York.

All three major U.S. stock indexes ended lower for the week as well.

data.title: Ambow Education Announces Second Half and Full-Year 2024 Financial Results
data.description:
CUPERTINO, Calif., March 28, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Ambow Education Holding Ltd. (NYSE American: AMBO) (“Ambow” or the “Company”), an AI-driven educational technology company, today announced its financial and operating results for the 2024 second half1 and full fiscal year,2 ended December 31, 2024.

“In 2024, we achieved full-year profitability and reached key milestones that position us for sustained long-term growth, driven by our sharpened focus on HybriU adoption,” said Dr. Jin Huang, Ambow’s President, Chief Executive Officer, and acting Chief Financial Officer. “We increased revenue, improved margins and strengthened profitability throughout the year, highlighted by a $1.3 million HybriU licensing agreement in the fourth quarter—marking our continued expansion into international markets.”

HybriU is a cutting-edge, AI-powered phygital (physical + digital) innovation that transforms education, corporate conferencing and events by seamlessly integrating the physical and digital worlds for a smarter, more immersive experience. Designed to bridge the gap between in-person and remote interaction, HybriU delivers real-time AI automation, immersive engagement and intelligent collaboration across industries.

In the education sector, HybriU offers the only patented, plug-and-play solution that seamlessly integrates lecture capture, connectivity, AI, immersive technologies and big data analytics. This all-in-one platform simplifies deployment while delivering a rich, connected and data-informed phygital learning experience.

“Looking ahead to 2025, we will accelerate HybriU’s adoption across U.S. and international markets and further enhance our AI capabilities to deliver greater value to our partners. With a strong financial foundation, a lean operational structure, favorable AI tailwinds and a differentiated first-to-market solution, we are well-positioned to drive continued growth and increasing profitability,” Dr. Huang concluded.

Fourth Quarter 2024 Financial Highlights

  • Net revenues in the fourth quarter of 2024 were $3.5 million, compared with $2.4 million in the same period of 2023. The increase was primarily due to the launch of HybriU.
  • Gross profit in the fourth quarter of 2024 was $2.3 million, compared with $1.2 million in the same period of 2023. Gross profit margin was 65.7%, compared with 50.0% in the fourth quarter of 2023.
  • Operating expenses in the fourth quarter of 2024 decreased by 13.3% to $1.3 million from $1.5 million in the same period of 2023. The decrease was primarily due to reduction in shared center expenses.
  • Operating income in the fourth quarter of 2024 improved to $1.0 million, compared with an operating loss of $0.3 million in the same period of 2023.
  • Net income attributable to the Company’s ordinary shareholders was $1.3 million, or $0.02 per basic and diluted share for the fourth quarter of 2024 and 2023.

1 Financial results for the second half of 2024 have not been audited or reviewed by the Company’s independent registered accounting firm.

2 Financial results for the full fiscal year ended December 31, 2024 have been audited by the Company’s independent registered accounting firm.

Fiscal Year 2024 Financial Highlights

  • Net revenues in fiscal year 2024 increased by 2.2% to $9.4 million from $9.2 million in 2023. The increase was primarily driven by revenue growth from the launch of HybriU, while partially offset by the closure of Bay State College.
  • Gross profit in fiscal year 2024 was $5.0 million, increasing from $2.5 million in 2023. The increase was primarily attributable to an increase in net revenues from HybriU and a reduction in payroll expenses and teaching costs upon the closure of Bay State College.
  • Operating expenses in fiscal year 2024 decreased by 16.2% to $5.7 million from $6.8 million in 2023. The decrease was primarily driven by reduced payroll expenses following the closure of Bay State College.
  • Operating loss in fiscal year 2024 narrowed to $0.7 million, compared with a loss of $4.3 million in 2023.
  • Net income attributable to the Company’s ordinary shareholders in fiscal year 2024 was $0.3 million, or $0.005 per basic and diluted share, compared with a net loss of $3.2 million, or $0.06 per basic and diluted share in 2023.
  • As of December 31, 2024, Ambow maintained solid cash resources of $8.4 million, including cash and cash equivalents of $1.1 million and restricted cash of $7.3 million.

Contingencies

We are currently involved in two lawsuits concerning our leased property. Filed on July 15, 2024, by Art Block Investors, LLC et al., in the San Diego Superior Court (the “Court”), this unlawful detainer action seeks possession of premises occupied by NewSchool and recovery of $2,255,984.44 in past rent and common area maintenance (CAM) fees. Following trial, the Court issued a Proposed Statement of Decision awarding the plaintiffs possession and damages, with attorney's fees and costs (estimated $80,000–$100,000) to be determined. NewSchool has objected, but judgment is expected within 30 days, followed by a motion for fees. In addition, filed on September 6, 2024, in the San Diego Superior Court, Art Block Investors, LLC et al. alleges breach of contract and guaranty against NewSchool and Ambow Education Holdings Ltd., seeking $4,466,247.80, potentially offset by amounts recovered in the first lawsuit. We, as defendants, have answered and are contesting the claims; no pretrial or trial dates have been set. The Company continues to evaluate these matters. A reasonable estimate of the amount of any possible loss or range of loss cannot be made as of December 31, 2024.

About Ambow

Ambow Education Holding Ltd. is a U.S.-based, AI-driven technology company offering phygital (physical + digital) solutions for education, corporate conferencing and live events. Through its flagship platform, HybriU, Ambow is shaping the future of learning, collaboration and communication—delivering immersive, intelligent and real-time experiences across industries. For more information, visit Ambow’s corporate website at https://www.ambow.com/.

Brazil's top court tosses out probe into Bolsonaro's vaccine records
data.description:
Brazilian Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes on Friday tossed out a probe into whether former President Jair Bolsonaro forged vaccine records, a court decision seen by Reuters showed.

Moraes' ruling came after Prosecutor General Paulo Gonet earlier this week decided not to charge Bolsonaro with fraud, saying he could not press charges based only on allegations from a plea-bargain deal made with a former presidential aide.

The move comes days after a Supreme Court panel voted to put Bolsonaro on trial as part of another case, in which he is accused of allegedly conspiring to overthrow the government after he lost the 2022 election.

Bolsonaro's lawyers did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

Moraes in his decision agreed with Gonet, emphasizing that Brazilian law does not allow charges to be presented based solely on a plea-bargain deal.

Criminal charges, the justice wrote, must be supported by "autonomous and independent evidence, in addition to information arising from plea-bargain deals duly ratified by other evidence."

Federal Police had formally accused Bolsonaro last year of fraud on his vaccination records, saying former aide Mauro Cid fraudulently obtained COVID-19 vaccination records for Bolsonaro and his daughter Laura at the request of the then-president.

data.title: These Stocks Moved the Most Today: Tesla, AppLovin, Lululemon, Nvidia, CoreWeave, Wolfspeed, and More
data.description:
Joe Woelfel and Mackenzie Tatananni

Stocks were declining sharply Friday after fresh data revealed U.S. core inflation rose more than expected in February and consumer sentiment dropped for a third-straight month. The S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average declined 2% and 1.7%, respectively, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite fell 2.6%.

These stocks were making moves Friday:

Tesla was down 3% following the release of the consumer-sentiment data. The stock closed Thursday with a gain of 0.4% in what was a tough session for auto makers following President Donald Trump's announcement he would be levying 25% tariffs on all cars and certain car parts made outside of the U.S. Tesla, an electric-vehicle manufacturer, edged higher because all the cars it sells in the U.S. are made in America. But General Motors, Ford Motor, and Jeep maker Stellantis don't, and their stocks declined Thursday. On Friday, GM fell 1.7%, Ford was off 2.4%, and Stellantis fell 4.3%.

Nvidia was down 1.2% after the maker of artificial-intelligence chips fell 2.1% on Thursday on concerns over its business in China.

Meanwhile, CoreWeave, the Nvidia-backed cloud-services provider, opened for trading Friday at $39 a share, below its initial-public-offering price of $40 a share, well below estimates for $47 to $55. At last check, the stock was up 2.2% at $40.89. CoreWeave is a pure play on AI, with all of its revenue coming from cloud rentals of AI servers that use Nvidia chips.

AppLovin was up 4.3%, a day after shares of the advertising-technology company slumped 20% following a report from short-seller Muddy Waters Research that it had taken a short position in the stock. In late February, short-sellers Fuzzy Panda Research and Culper Research also issued negative reports on the company. AppLovin CEO Adam Foroughi said at the time in a blog post that it was "disappointing that a few nefarious short-sellers are making false and misleading claims aimed at undermining our success."

Lululemon Athletica dove 16% after the activewear company reported fourth-quarter earnings that topped Wall Street expectations but fiscal-year guidance came up short, according to FactSet. Lululemon expects sales for the fiscal year ending in January of $11.15 billion to $11.3 billion, representing growth of 5% to 7%. However, analysts had been projecting a revenue increase of 7% to $11.3 billion. CEO Calvin McDonald said the company believes "the dynamic macro environment has contributed to a more cautious consumer."

Wolfspeed cratered 50%. The maker of EV chips said it "maintains constructive dialogue with the White House, its legislators, and the U.S. Department of Commerce to secure federal funding and on ways Wolfspeed can support the Trump administration's efforts to reinforce U.S. industrial leadership in semiconductors." The Triangle Business Journal, according to a report from Dow Jones Newswires, said interim Executive Chairman Thomas Werner told reporters that a $750 million funding agreement secured by the company likely will evolve "because the company has evolved and because the chip office is different, there's a new administration."

United States Steel rose 0.4% after falling earlier in the session. The stock wavered on the heels of a report that said the company's $14 billion merger with Japan's Nippon Steel could still be saved. The two companies are in talks with President Trump, with Nippon Steel offering to more than double an investment in U.S. Steel facilities to $7 billion from $2.7 billion, Semafor reported.

Braze was rising 3.5% after the cloud-based software company reported fiscal fourth-quarter earnings that beat analysts' estimates and issued better-than-expected fiscal-year guidance.

Oxford Industries declined 5.5% after the owner of Tommy Bahama and Lilly Pulitzer apparel issued revenue forecasts for its fiscal first quarter and year that missed analysts' expectations. Following a strong finish to calendar 2024, "trends moderated in January as there was less of a reason to shop, a pattern we've witnessed for the past several quarters, as well as a deterioration in consumer sentiment that also weighed on demand," Chairman and CEO Tom Chubb said in a statement.

Rocket Lab USA gained 2.3% after the maker of space-launch technology was selected by the U.S. Space Force to compete for a contract with a maximum value of $5.6 billion. The contract, as part of the National Security Space Launch Phase 3 Lane 1 program, runs through June 2029, and includes an option for an additional five years.

Argan, the energy facility construction company, soared 20% after reporting fiscal fourth-quarter earnings and revenue that rose from a year earlier. Driving gains was the company's power industry services segment, Argan said.

AAR posted strong fiscal-third-quarter adjusted earnings, but revenue missed forecasts, sending shares of the aviation-service company down 15%.

Write to Joe Woelfel at joseph.woelfel@barrons.com and Mackenzie Tatananni at mackenzie.tatananni@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: TSX Close: The Index Loses 400 Pts as a Trade War With the U.S. is Set To Intensify
data.description:
The Toronto Stock Exchange fell more than 400 points or 1.5% on Friday as President Donald Trump said "things are going to work out" between Canada and the United States, even as he looks set to press ahead with a costly all-out tariff war between the two nations that is set to intensify next week.

The S&P/TSX Composite Index fell 401.91 points to 24,759.15, to complete a losing week for the index. Most sectors were lower, led by Base Metals, down 3.7%, and Info Tech, down near 3%. In contrast, Utilities were up about 0.2%.

At the end of a week that saw him impose tariff on U.S. automobile imports, including those from Canada, and also threaten coming tariffs on lumber and copper among other things, in addition to tariffs already on steel and aluminum. Still, Trump on Friday afternoon said: "I think things are going to work out very well between Canada the United States."

CTVNews noted that Trump, when speaking about the upcoming reciprocal tariffs set to be imposed on all countries with levies against the United States., claimed "many countries" are taking advantage of the country, adding with a caveat: "I'm not referring to Canada," he said, before later adding, "We're going to end up very good relationship with Canada."

But CTVNews noted Trump then, despite his encouraging tone, also said he would still "absolutely follow through" with his promise to impose further tariffs on Canadian goods.

Meanwhile, the prime minister's website published this on the talks between Carney and Trump: "The leaders agreed to begin comprehensive negotiations about a new economic and security relationship immediately following the election. In the interim, the leaders agreed that conversations between the Minister of International Trade and Intergovernmental Affairs and President of the King's Privy Council for Canada, Dominic LeBlanc, and the United States Secretary of Commerce, Howard Lutnick, will intensify to address immediate concerns."

It added: "The Prime Minister informed the President that his government will implement retaliatory tariffs to protect Canadian workers and our economy, following the announcement of additional U.S. trade actions on April 2."

Avery Shenfeld, chief economist at CIBC Capital Markets, on Friday looked at how Canada should respond to the U.S. auto tariffs. According to Shenfeld, one item on the "not to do" list would be for Canada to apply its own 25% tariff on U.S. vehicle parts for which there is no immediate capacity for a made-in-Canada substitute. "That would be essentially self-defeating, as it would magnify the cost disadvantage of a Canadian-assembled vehicle that then faced tariffs in the US."

What, Shenfeld asked himself, about a 25% tariff on fully assembled vehicles shipped from the US? Shenfeld said that didn't show up on the list of proposed measures offered by the current government, and with good reason as it would significantly add to inflation in Canada, and would favor vehicles from overseas suppliers with no Canadian content over US-built vehicles that will still have some made in Canada components for now. Moreover, a tariff on U.S. cars and trucks would pose a risk that Trump would just add to the tariff on those assembled in Canada, he added.

Canada, Shenfeld said, could offer incentives, perhaps in tax policies, aimed at encouraging manufacturers to continue to operate their vehicle or plants on this side of the border. He added: "That might make sense while we retain the hope that these US tariffs won't be permanent. Such incentives needn't cover the full hit such vehicles will bear from tariffs, given that car companies are already going to be reluctant to spend billions of dollars to reshore all of their production as long as the long term future of tariff policy is still in doubt. But there will need to be some care to fashion such incentives in a way that doesn't lead the US to call them a subsidy and respond with even higher tariffs."

More broadly, Shenfeld noted Canada will also be thinking about its response to next week's announced US 'reciprocal tariff', which if calculated fairly, might replace the previously-threatened 25% tariff on all products (10% on energy and potash) with something milder. "Here again," he said, "counter-tariffs might be effective, but also risk a retaliation to our retaliation, as we saw with the threatened surcharge on Ontario's electricity exports."

An alternative, or at least a complementary measure, would be to let Canadians apply their own penalty on U.S. exports, through the choices they are making at the supermarket, in the shopping mall, at their car dealer, and at the travel agency, Shenfeld noted.

"While it's too soon for that to show up in the data, we're getting anecdotal evidence from a broad range of clients that Canadians are taking action in their purchases and vacations, not always to buy Canadian, but to avoid the US. So far, that's only a grassroots movement without a leader and a bullhorn. But it could be ramped up through publicly-funded marketing campaigns, or even better, targeted ads by Canadian goods suppliers, retailers and tourist agencies," he wrote.

Shenfeld added, "That's not something that the Trump administration can point at finger at our government and retaliate against. Choosing not to buy something is the right of every citizen in a free society, and if joined by Mexican, European and Asian countries, could apply more pressure on the US economy, and its political leaders, than tariffs alone. Plan B for Canada entails a long and costly re-orientation of our economy to reduce its reliance on goods exports to the US. But Plan A, getting those tariffs removed or sharply reduced at the negotiating table, by creating enough hardship in US output and inflation, is still our best bet."

Of commodities, gold traded at yet another record high late afternoon on Friday as investors move to the metal's safe haven amid Trump's tariff wars, while a key U.S. inflation measure rose more than expected last month. Gold for June delivery was last seen up $24.40 to US$3,115.30 per ounce.

But West Texas Intermediate crude oil closed lower as markets were broadly weaker ahead of fresh tariffs expected next week. WTI crude oil for May delivery closed down $0.56 to settle at US$69.36 per barrel, while May Brent crude was last seen down $0.38 to US$73.65.

(TSX: EQX; NYSE:EQX) (“Equinox”), Calibre has filed and commenced mailing of its management information circular (the “Circular”) and related materials (collectively, the “Meeting Materials”) for the upcoming special meeting (the “Meeting”) of holders of Calibre common shares (the “Calibre Shareholders”) and holders of options issued under Calibre’s amended and restated long-term incentive plan (the “Calibre Optionholders”, and together with the Calibre Shareholders, the “Calibre Securityholders”).

The Meeting Materials are now available under Calibre’s profile on SEDAR+ at www.sedarplus.ca and on the Company’s website at Calibre Mining - April 24, 2025 Special Meeting of Calibre Securityholders. Calibre Securityholders of record as of the close of business on March 18, 2025, will receive the Meeting Materials by mail and are encouraged to review them carefully.

Meeting Details

The Meeting will be held on Thursday, April 24, 2025, at 10:00 a.m. (Vancouver time) at the offices of Cassels Brock & Blackwell LLP, Suite 2200, RBC Place, 885 West Georgia Street, Vancouver, British Columbia. The Meeting will be held in-person, although Calibre Securityholders can also access the Meeting via live webcast at meetnow.global/MZLUU6Z. Only Calibre Securityholders who are present in person and entitled to vote at the Meeting are able to vote during the Meeting. Any Calibre Securityholder attending the Meeting via the live webcast will not be able to vote at the Meeting.

At the Meeting, Calibre Securityholders will be asked to consider, and if deemed advisable, to pass a special resolution (the “Arrangement Resolution”) to approve the Arrangement under Part 9, Division 5 of the Business Corporations Act (British Columbia), whereby Equinox will (among other things) acquire all of the issued and outstanding common shares of Calibre in accordance with the arrangement agreement dated February 23, 2025 (the “Arrangement Agreement”).

Under the terms of the Arrangement, each Calibre share will be exchanged for 0.31 of an Equinox common share. If the Arrangement is completed, existing Equinox and former Calibre shareholders (including former holders of restricted share units and performance share units of Calibre) will own approximately 63% and 37%, respectively, of the combined company.

Interim Order & Advance Ruling Certificate

The Company is pleased to announce that the Supreme Court of British Columbia (the “Court”) has issued an interim order (the “Interim Order”) regarding the Arrangement which authorizes Calibre to proceed with the Meeting and addresses other Meeting related matters. A copy of the Interim Order is included in the Circular. Subject to receipt of the requisite approvals by Calibre Securityholders at the Meeting, it is expected that Calibre will apply for a final order of the Court approving the Arrangement on April 29, 2025.

In addition, on March 25, 2025, an advance ruling certificate (“ARC”) was received from the Commission of Competition pursuant to section 102 of the Competition Act whereby the Commission does not intend to make an application with respect to the Transaction. This ARC satisfies the Canadian Competition Approval closing condition to the Arrangement. Assuming the satisfaction or waiver of all of the conditions to the completion of the Arrangement, the Arrangement is anticipated to close by the end of Q2 2025.

Strategic Rationale

Combining Calibre and Equinox will create:

  • A major diversified gold producer in the Americas: Potential for more than 1.2 million ounces of annual gold production1 from a portfolio of mines in five countries in the Americas.
  • The second largest gold producer in Canada: Greenstone Gold Mine and Valentine Gold Mine, two new long-life, low-cost, open-pit gold mines, are expected to produce collectively 590,000 ounces of gold per year2 when at capacity.
  • Substantial free cash flow: Immediate increase in production at record high gold prices is expected to drive superior free cash flow to enable the combined company to quickly deleverage.
  • Exceptional growth profile: Additional production growth from the ramp-up of Valentine Gold Mine and a pipeline of development and expansion projects.
  • Significant re-rate potential based on valuation of peers: Greater scale, lower risk, near-term production growth, and superior free cash flow relative to peers, providing significant revaluation potential.
  • An industry-leading team: Proven track record of delivery and shareholder value creation led by Ross Beaty, and Blayne Johnson and Doug Forster of Featherstone Capital, who will all serve on the board of directors of the combined company. In total, four directors of Calibre will serve on the board of directors of the combined company, and Darren Hall, the President and Chief Executive Officer of Calibre, will serve as the President and Chief Operating Officer of the combined company with full responsibility of the combined operations going forward.

Board Recommendation

The board of directors of Calibre (the “Board”) have unanimously determined that the Arrangement is in the best interests of Calibre and recommend that Calibre Securityholders vote FOR the Arrangement Resolution.

Calibre Securityholders are encouraged to carefully read the Circular in its entirety and vote as soon as possible in accordance with the instructions accompanying the applicable form of proxy or voting instruction form mailed to Calibre Securityholders together with the Circular. The deadline for voting by proxy is 10:00 a.m. (Vancouver time) on April 22, 2025.

How to Vote

Shareholder Questions and Voting Assistance

If you have any questions or need assistance voting your shares, please contact Calibre’s proxy solicitation agent:

Does not include any production from Equinox’s Los Filos Gold Mine or either company’s expansion projects.

2 Average annual production as estimated in the most recent technical reports for each project, which are available for download on each company’s website and on SEDAR+.

Qualified Person

The scientific and technical information contained in this news release was approved by David Schonfeldt P.Geo., Calibre’s Corporate Chief Geologist and a "Qualified Person" under National Instrument 43-101.

About Calibre

Calibre is a Canadian-listed, Americas focused, growing mid-tier gold producer with a strong pipeline of development and exploration opportunities across Newfoundland & Labrador in Canada, Nevada and Washington in the USA, and Nicaragua. Calibre is focused on delivering sustainable value for shareholders, local communities and all stakeholders through responsible operations and a disciplined approach to growth. With a strong balance sheet, a proven management team, strong operating cash flow, accretive development projects and district-scale exploration opportunities, Calibre will unlock significant value.

Non-IFRS Financial Measures

Calibre has included certain non-IFRS measures as discussed below. The Company believes that these measures, in addition to conventional measures prepared in accordance with IFRS, provide investors with an improved ability to evaluate the underlying performance of the Company. These non-IFRS measures are intended to provide additional information and should not be considered in isolation or as a substitute for measures of performance prepared in accordance with IFRS. These measures do not have any standardized meaning prescribed under IFRS, and therefore may not be comparable to other issuers.

Free Cash Flow

Free cash flow subtracts sustaining capital expenditures from net cash provided by operating activities, serving as an indicator of the capacity to generate cash from operations post-sustaining capital investments.
